name: Indian Mackerel
description: A staple in wet markets, commonly grilled or fried. Recognized for its affordability and rich flavor.

name: Flying Fish
description: Known for gliding above the water surface, this fish is unique to deeper open waters and caught near the Sulu Sea and parts of eastern Bicol.

name: Threadfin Bream
description: A common market fish in Bicol with a pinkish hue and mild, sweet-tasting meat, frequently used in soups or grilled dishes.

name: Rabbitfish
description: A popular breakfast fish, often dried and paired with vinegar or garlic rice. Found in shallow coastal waters and coral reefs.

name: Irrawaddy Dolphin
description: A critically endangered dolphin species that inhabits coastal waters and estuaries. While their presence might be less widely known than whale sharks, they have been reported in certain areas of the Philippines.
location: Coastal estuaries and waters of the Bicol region

name: Stingray
description: Large, graceful filter-feeding rays known for their wing-like pectoral fins. They are often sighted in the waters around the Bicol region, including near Ticao Island.
location: Waters off Ticao Island (Masbate), coastal areas of Albay and Sorsogon

name: Sea Turtles
description: Endangered marine reptiles that nest on the beaches and forage in the seagrass beds and coral reefs of the Bicol region.
location: Nesting beaches and coastal waters of Albay and Sorsogon

name: Squid
description: A widely consumed cephalopod in the region, often grilled or used in adobo. Actively fished in the deeper parts of the Bicol seas.

name: Tabios
description: Known as the world's smallest commercially harvested fish, this tiny species is endemic to Lake Buhi in Camarines Sur and is considered a delicacy.

name: Giant Clams
description: Large bivalve mollusks that inhabit coral reefs. Several species can be found in the reef ecosystems of the Bicol region, some of which are endangered.
location: Coral reefs of Albay and Sorsogon

name: Sardine
description: Small, oily fish that often swim in large schools. They are a significant part of the marine food web and are widely consumed, often canned.
location: Aklan, Antique, Leyte, Samar, Manila Bay

name: Tiki-tiki
description: Tiny schooling fish widely used in making bagoong or dried fish products. Common in coastal Bicol towns.

name: Mackerel Tuna
description: A medium-sized tuna species that’s important to regional fisheries. Known for its strong flavor and is often used in local dishes like sinaing or paksiw.

name: Bangus
description: The milkfish is widely farmed in brackish ponds and considered the national fish of the Philippines.
location: Brackish fishponds and coastal waters of Bicol.

name: Tilapia
description: A freshwater fish introduced for aquaculture, known for fast growth and easy farming.

name: Dalag
description: Mudfish with firm white flesh, commonly cooked in soups or grilled.
location: Swamps, rice paddies, and freshwater bodies.

name: Hito
description: Popular catfish species resilient in various freshwater conditions.
location: Freshwater lakes, rivers, and fish farms.

name: Karpa
description: Common carp introduced for aquaculture, thriving in ponds and lakes.

name: Gurami
description: Snakeskin gourami often found in freshwater habitats and aquaculture.

name: Ayungin
description: Endemic silver perch valued for its mild taste.

name: Biya
description: Small goby species found in brackish and freshwater environments.
location: Estuaries, river mouths, and coastal waters.

name: Ulang
description: Giant freshwater prawn known for its sweet, tender meat.

name: Alumahan
description: A small mackerel species rich in flavor and oil.

name: Galunggong
description: Round scad, a staple fish in local markets for everyday meals.

name: Tulingan
description: Small tuna species often used in traditional dishes.

name: Tanigue
description: Spanish mackerel prized for its firm flesh and flavor.

name: Lapu-lapu
description: Groupers valued for their firm, tasty meat.

name: Pampano
description: A fish known for its mild flavor and tender flesh.

name: Bariles
description: Yellowfin tuna prized in commercial and local fisheries.

name: Pusit
description: Squid commonly harvested and used in various recipes.

name: Hipon
description: Shrimp species popular in Filipino seafood dishes.

name: Alimango
description: Mud crab prized for its sweet meat, often steamed or cooked in coconut milk.

name: Alimasag
description: Blue swimmer crab common in coastal fisheries.

name: Manila Sea Catfish
description: Marine catfish found in brackish and coastal waters.

name: Speckled Goby
description: A small goby species found in freshwater and brackish areas.

name: White Sardine
description: A small schooling fish used in dried or canned products.

name: Bloch's Gizzard Shad
description: Common fish near river mouths and brackish water.

name: Longspine Ponyfish
description: Bioluminescent fish found in shallow coastal waters.

name: Tawilis
description: The only freshwater sardine endemic to the Philippines.

name: Maliputo
description: Freshwater trevally known for firm flesh and flavor.

name: Tikos Rough Triggerfish
description: Triggerfish inhabiting reefs with rough skin.

name: Picturesque Dragonet
description: Colorful reef fish famous for vivid patterns and bright colors.

name: Arahan Roughback Sea Catfish
description: Sea catfish commonly found in coastal and estuarine waters.
